mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2008.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and capital investment, forcing firms to cut production and guidance, which drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or positive returns dominate during synchronized downturns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the index of industrial production following a sharp drawdown reflects restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lifts corporate output and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO recoveries do not yield positive 5-day forward returns more than 50 percent of the time, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 215.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the inflation threshold shows zero predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, thereby generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue streams, which directly translates into stronger earnings forecasts and positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 110.0 signals severe manufacturing weakness and capacity underutilization, which depresses industrial sector earnings and drags down broader equity indices over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO drops below 110.0, or the threshold trigger loses statistical significance.
